step_by_step_thinking: I believe the correct answer remains C (HDL), based on the evidence provided earlier. Here's why: while LDL carries cholesterol to peripheral tissues, HDL plays an essential role in clearing excess cholesterol from these areas, particularly arterial walls. By doing so, HDL prevents the accumulation of fatty plaques that can lead to atherosclerosis—a major cause of heart disease.
raw_response: I believe the correct answer remains C (HDL), based on the evidence provided earlier. Here's why: while LDL carries cholesterol to peripheral tissues, HDL plays an essential role in clearing excess cholesterol from these areas, particularly arterial walls. By doing so, HDL prevents the accumulation of fatty plaques that can lead to atherosclerosis—a major cause of heart disease. Therefore, although all four options relate to cholesterol transportation, HDL's specialized function in reverse cholesterol transport makes it uniquely significant in maintaining vascular health.
